critic_raw:
Single-pass verdict — churn model:

The churn model may be better than the incumbent, but the evaluation does not demonstrate this for the actual use case. The 2% capacity constraint makes this a selective-intervention problem, and the evaluation metric (global ROC-AUC) does not measure selective-intervention quality.

Key gaps:
- No precision@2% or lift@2% reported
- No incremental retention outcome measured
- AUC gains in irrelevant score ranges may account for the full improvement

The case for an immediate workflow switch is not established. The team should compute top-2% metrics from the existing test data before making a production change.

Verdict: empirical_test_agreed
adjudication_raw: Single-agent baseline run 3. All three must-find issues identified. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

empirical_test.measure: top-2% precision and lift on held-out test month
empirical_test.success_criterion: new model precision and lift at 2% cutoff are higher than incumbent's
empirical_test.failure_criterion: top-2% metrics are not improved for the new model despite higher AUC
